external_changes.dome_site_changes: No new changes this run. PRED-105 silent-ignore continues. Existing external report-1.json (reader praise, logged no-action) already tracked.

patches_self_applied: WIN-040 detail_evidence: Prepended new opening paragraph engaging 45°W threshold, 80°W cell centre, drift rate, >100-year falsifiability horizon, and structural axial-symmetry argument (H(r) zero azimuthal derivative). Via EXP-226 tracker.
WIN-040 detail_verdict_text: Replaced generic post-hoc boilerplate with WIN-040-specific sentence naming WMM-2025/CHAOS-7 and 45°W threshold. Via EXP-226 tracker.

WIN-053 detail_evidence: Replaced third-horn closure sentence. 'parameterless' → 'three-parameter surface fit — with none of A, C, or λ derived from dome geometry'. 'does no predictive work' → 'does no numerical predictive work beyond dictating two peaks'. Via EXP-225 tracker.

WIN-068 finding: Replaced retracted 'empirically calibrated per-station' claim with accurate framing: dome equation is real (FSF=H(r)/r) but absolute magnitudes use observation-anchored baseline. Via c9 critical hole fix.
WIN-068 detail_evidence: Reframed 'by contrast' as concession-then-pivot — lead with legitimate geometric derivation, then pivot to observation-anchored baseline.
WIN-061 detail_evidence: Fixed DOI 10.1029/1999JA900037 → 10.1029/1999JA900056 (both occurrences). Correct DOI resolves to Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 JGR paper on storm-SR coupling.
WIN-010 finding: Updated to cite Kim & Chang (2018) alongside Chapman 1933, removing overstatement that Chapman 'predicts exact signal magnitude'.
SEC-8b.3 html: Fixed 'Pass and Fail become mutually exclusive' → 'Pass and Fail overlap on the same observable' (corrected logical inversion).
exps_created: EXP-227 (WIN-061 V51.1 full rewrite, high priority)

internal_issues.description: Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 citation misrepresented. Paper found ENHANCEMENT (increased frequency, decreased damping), not suppression. Our text cites it to support 'suppression is standard physics'. DOI fix applied but full paragraph rewrite needed.

Mechanism description physically backwards. 'storm-injected particles increase D-region conductivity, lowering cavity Q' contradicts what Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 measured (decreased damping = increased Q).

analyst_id_swap_note: EXP-225-WIN040 and EXP-226-WIN053 file names were swapped vs tracker IDs. Tracker EXP-225 targets WIN-053 and was completed by the EXP-226-WIN053 file. Tracker EXP-226 targets WIN-040 and was completed by the EXP-225-WIN040 file. Both integrated correctly; issue IDs matched by content, not file name.
